{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LqNznjfcG4W4pBE4b7ThfW","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"aa97615c-afc5-4181-8498-6d1bb3c742fb","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:00:08.698Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01M7oCzmSwzrSEumSy6B6dfp","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Agent Memory\n     2→\n     3→## Project\n     4→- **Name**: Bookmate\n     5→- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     6→- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n     7→- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n     8→- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n     9→\n    10→## Current State\n    11→- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n    12→- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n    13→- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n    14→- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n    15→- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n    16→- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    17→- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n    18→- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n    19→- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n    20→- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n    21→- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n    22→- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n    23→- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n    24→  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n    25→  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n    26→  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n    27→  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n    28→  - Skip-to-content link\n    29→  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n    30→  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n    31→  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n    32→\n    33→## Production\n    34→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    35→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n    36→- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    37→- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n    38→- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    39→- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n    40→- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n    41→- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n    42→\n    43→## Open PRs\n    44→- None\n    45→\n    46→## Closed Recently\n    47→- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n    48→- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n    49→- #72: Test coverage for profile genres, reading, reputation routes — merged\n    50→- #71: BookCover component with error fallback — merged + deployed\n    51→- #70: Auto-hide listings with past start dates — merged + deployed\n    52→\n    53→## Open Issues\n    54→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n    55→- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~4 days left\n    56→\n    57→## Important Notes\n    58→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    59→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    60→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    61→- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    62→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n    63→- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n    64→- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n    65→- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n    66→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    67→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    68→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    69→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    70→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    71→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    72→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    73→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    74→- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n    75→- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n    76→- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n    77→- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n    78→\n    79→## Test Suite\n    80→- 126 tests across 14 test files, all passing\n    81→- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit\n    82→- Not yet tested: telegram, discord routes\n    83→- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n    84→- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n    85→\n    86→## Components\n    87→- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n    88→\n    89→## Next Session Priority\n    90→1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n    91→2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n    92→3. Check production health\n    93→4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements\n Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n- #72: Test coverage for profile genres, reading, reputation routes — merged\n- #71: BookCover component with error fallback — merged + deployed\n- #70: Auto-hide listings with past start dates — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~4 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 126 tests across 14 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit\n- Not yet tested: telegram, discord routes\n- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements\n","numLines":94,"startLine":1,"totalLines":94}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0119qEdsShHsWRqfiRozcXc4","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-23 21:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 5: found and fixed 4 bugs across notifications, platform link API, applications validation, and React hooks. Created issue #82, implemented fixes in PR #83, merged and deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 128 tests pass\n     8→- **PM2 logs**: \"Failed to find Server Action 'x'\" errors — investigated and confirmed as external probing (app has zero Server Actions). Not a bug.\n     9→- **CI**: Green on main\n    10→- **Open PRs**: None\n    11→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    12→- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→\n    16→### Issue #82: Code review round 5 (PR #83 — merged & deployed)\n    17→\n    18→4 bugs fixed:\n    19→\n    20→1. **Author receives duplicate notifications when group fills** — `notifyGroupFull` included the author in `listing_members`, so author got both `new_member` and `group_full` notifications. Fixed by excluding author_id from the members query.\n    21→\n    22→2. **Platform links settable before group is full** — Telegram and Discord link-setting API routes had no `is_full` check. Author could store links via API before the group filled, bypassing the intended flow. Added `is_full` guard returning 400.\n    23→\n    24→3. **applicationId not validated as integer** — PATCH applications accepted non-numeric values silently, producing confusing 404s. Added explicit positive integer validation.\n    25→\n    26→4. **fetchListing not wrapped in useCallback** — ESLint exhaustive-deps warning was suppressed. Wrapped in `useCallback` with proper `[id]` dependency and removed suppression comment.\n    27→\n    28→**Testing:** 128 tests pass, ESLint clean, build passes. Deployed and verified healthy.\n    29→\n    30→## Issues Monitored\n    31→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n    32→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    33→\n    34→## Next Session Priority\n    35→1. Check production health\n    36→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    37→3. Look for new improvements or issues\n    38→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"ab78fa7a-90c6-4f43-b922-055ccae79bed","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:00:16.469Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-23-2100.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-23 21:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 5: found and fixed 4 bugs across notifications, platform link API, applications validation, and React hooks. Created issue #82, implemented fixes in PR #83, merged and deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 128 tests pass\n- **PM2 logs**: \"Failed to find Server Action 'x'\" errors — investigated and confirmed as external probing (app has zero Server Actions). Not a bug.\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #82: Code review round 5 (PR #83 — merged & deployed)\n\n4 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **Author receives duplicate notifications when group fills** — `notifyGroupFull` included the author in `listing_members`, so author got both `new_member` and `group_full` notifications. Fixed by excluding author_id from the members query.\n\n2. **Platform links settable before group is full** — Telegram and Discord link-setting API routes had no `is_full` check. Author could store links via API before the group filled, bypassing the intended flow. Added `is_full` guard returning 400.\n\n3. **applicationId not validated as integer** — PATCH applications accepted non-numeric values silently, producing confusing 404s. Added explicit positive integer validation.\n\n4. **fetchListing not wrapped in useCallback** — ESLint exhaustive-deps warning was suppressed. Wrapped in `useCallback` with proper `[id]` dependency and removed suppression comment.\n\n**Testing:** 128 tests pass, ESLint clean, build passes. Deployed and verified healthy.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Look for new improvements or issues\n","numLines":38,"startLine":1,"totalLines":38}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013zrUQYFUWvRRa1zcBtucER","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-23 20:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 4: found and fixed 6 bugs across registration, listing API, rate limiter, and date filtering. Created issue #80, implemented fixes in PR #81, merged and deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, API responsive, PM2 online\n     8→- **PM2 logs**: Showed restart loop between 18:03-19:09 UTC (build dir missing during previous deploy), but recovered by 19:09 UTC\n     9→- **CI**: Green on main\n    10→- **Open PRs**: None\n    11→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    12→- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), unrelated NomadLingo issue\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→\n    16→### Issue #80: Code review round 4 (PR #81 — merged & deployed)\n    17→\n    18→6 bugs fixed:\n    19→\n    20→1. **Email duplicate check not normalized** — Registration used raw email for duplicate SELECT but stored lowercase. Case-variant emails bypass 409 and hit 500 from UNIQUE constraint. Fixed by normalizing before the check.\n    21→\n    22→2. **Application status hidden when requires_approval off** — GET listing only showed pending application status when `requires_approval` was true. If toggled off after someone applied, the UI showed a \"Join\" button instead of pending state. Removed the `requires_approval` guard.\n    23→\n    24→3. **Join without checking pending applications** — Users with pending applications could directly join if `requires_approval` was toggled off. Added pending application check to join route.\n    25→\n    26→4. **Rate limiter setInterval leak** — `setInterval` cleanup ran on every module import during hot reload, creating duplicate intervals. Guarded with global singleton pattern.\n    27→\n    28→5. **Platform link exposure to non-members** — `telegram_link` and `discord_link` returned in listing response to all visitors. Now stripped for non-members/non-authors.\n    29→\n    30→6. **Date filter double-apply** — Two conflicting `start_date >= ?` conditions when both `includePast=false` and `start_date_from` were set. Merged into single effective condition using the more restrictive date.\n    31→\n    32→**Testing:** 128 tests pass (2 new for pending application guard), ESLint clean, build passes. Deployed and verified healthy.\n    33→\n    34→## Issues Monitored\n    35→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n    36→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    37→\n    38→## Next Session Priority\n    39→1. Check production health\n    40→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    41→3. Look for new improvements or PRD sync needs\n    42→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"df68e67e-44bd-4959-822b-8b67a3276857","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:00:16.907Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-23-2000.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-23 20:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 4: found and fixed 6 bugs across registration, listing API, rate limiter, and date filtering. Created issue #80, implemented fixes in PR #81, merged and deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, API responsive, PM2 online\n- **PM2 logs**: Showed restart loop between 18:03-19:09 UTC (build dir missing during previous deploy), but recovered by 19:09 UTC\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), unrelated NomadLingo issue\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #80: Code review round 4 (PR #81 — merged & deployed)\n\n6 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **Email duplicate check not normalized** — Registration used raw email for duplicate SELECT but stored lowercase. Case-variant emails bypass 409 and hit 500 from UNIQUE constraint. Fixed by normalizing before the check.\n\n2. **Application status hidden when requires_approval off** — GET listing only showed pending application status when `requires_approval` was true. If toggled off after someone applied, the UI showed a \"Join\" button instead of pending state. Removed the `requires_approval` guard.\n\n3. **Join without checking pending applications** — Users with pending applications could directly join if `requires_approval` was toggled off. Added pending application check to join route.\n\n4. **Rate limiter setInterval leak** — `setInterval` cleanup ran on every module import during hot reload, creating duplicate intervals. Guarded with global singleton pattern.\n\n5. **Platform link exposure to non-members** — `telegram_link` and `discord_link` returned in listing response to all visitors. Now stripped for non-members/non-authors.\n\n6. **Date filter double-apply** — Two conflicting `start_date >= ?` conditions when both `includePast=false` and `start_date_from` were set. Merged into single effective condition using the more restrictive date.\n\n**Testing:** 128 tests pass (2 new for pending application guard), ESLint clean, build passes. Deployed and verified healthy.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Look for new improvements or PRD sync needs\n","numLines":42,"startLine":1,"totalLines":42}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013pPujs9JVG6JxkiQPcb73Y","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Testing\n    32→- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n    33→- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n    34→- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n    35→- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n    36→- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n    37→- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n    38→- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n    39→- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n    40→- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n    41→\n    42→## Browser Testing\n    43→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    44→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    45→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    46→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    47→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    48→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    49→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    50→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    51→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    52→\n    53→## Deployment\n    54→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    55→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    56→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    57→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    58→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    59→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    60→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    61→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    62→\n    63→## React + SQLite\n    64→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    65→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    66→\n    67→## Security\n    68→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    69→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    70→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    71→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    72→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    73→\n    74→## Working Directory\n    75→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    76→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    77→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"59f56bca-7f7c-41b2-98d8-83e8d80e2f73","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:00:17.018Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Testing\n- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LJ5EDhKocN8FjnYhXptjb8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { isBotConfigured, setWebhook, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     3→\n     4→/**\n     5→ * POST /api/telegram/setup\n     6→ *\n     7→ * Sets up the Telegram webhook. Should be called once after deployment.\n     8→ * Requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL env vars.\n     9→ *\n    10→ * Protected by a setup secret to prevent unauthorized access.\n    11→ */\n    12→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    13→  const setupSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET;\n    14→  if (!setupSecret) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json(\n    16→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET not configured\" },\n    17→      { status: 500 }\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const body = await req.json();\n    22→  if (body.secret !== setupSecret) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured\" },\n    29→      { status: 500 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const webhookUrl = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL;\n    34→  if (!webhookUrl) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL not configured\" },\n    37→      { status: 500 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    42→  if (!bot) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json(\n    44→      { error: \"Failed to connect to Telegram API. Check your bot token.\" },\n    45→      { status: 500 }\n    46→    );\n    47→  }\n    48→\n    49→  const success = await setWebhook(webhookUrl);\n    50→  if (!success) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Failed to set webhook\" },\n    53→      { status: 500 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  return NextResponse.json({\n    58→    ok: true,\n    59→    bot: { username: bot.username, id: bot.id },\n    60→    webhookUrl,\n    61→  });\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→/**\n    65→ * GET /api/telegram/setup\n    66→ *\n    67→ * Returns the current Telegram bot configuration status.\n    68→ */\n    69→export async function GET() {\n    70→  return NextResponse.json({\n    71→    botConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n    72→    webhookUrlConfigured: !!process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL,\n    73→  });\n    74→}\n    75→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"1643d5a6-68b6-4301-bdb0-62c6930ff5db","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:01:02.442Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Bnguv8VVknKEvUutFkhiKu","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { isBotConfigured, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     3→\n     4→/**\n     5→ * Discord bot setup endpoint.\n     6→ * Returns bot info and invite URL for verification.\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Protected by DISCORD_SETUP_SECRET env var.\n     9→ */\n    10→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    11→  const { secret } = await req.json();\n    12→  const setupSecret = process.env.DISCORD_SETUP_SECRET || \"\";\n    13→\n    14→  if (!setupSecret || secret !== setupSecret) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json(\n    16→      { error: \"Invalid setup secret\" },\n    17→      { status: 403 }\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    22→    return NextResponse.json(\n    23→      {\n    24→        error:\n    25→          \"Discord bot is not configured. Set DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID environment variables.\",\n    26→      },\n    27→      { status: 503 }\n    28→    );\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  const botInfo = await getBotInfo();\n    32→  if (!botInfo) {\n    33→    return NextResponse.json(\n    34→      { error: \"Failed to connect to Discord API. Check your bot token.\" },\n    35→      { status: 500 }\n    36→    );\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  return NextResponse.json({\n    40→    ok: true,\n    41→    bot: {\n    42→      id: botInfo.id,\n    43→      username: botInfo.username,\n    44→    },\n    45→    message: `Discord bot \"${botInfo.username}\" is configured and ready.`,\n    46→  });\n    47→}\n    48→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"537b85d5-7aa0-4134-ac18-50682e52e9ae","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:01:02.632Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JnHuHvk45SgNFTnASdRtnE","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateGroupDeepLink } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Returns the Telegram deep link for one-click group creation.\n    10→ * Only available to the listing author when the group is full\n    11→ * and no Telegram link has been set yet.\n    12→ */\n    13→export async function GET(\n    14→  _req: NextRequest,\n    15→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    16→) {\n    17→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    18→  if (!session) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    21→      { status: 401 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json(\n    27→      { error: \"Telegram bot not configured\", botConfigured: false },\n    28→      { status: 404 }\n    29→    );\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const { id } = await params;\n    33→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    34→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    36→  }\n    37→\n    38→  const listing = db\n    39→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    40→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    41→\n    42→  if (!listing) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"Only the organizer can create the Telegram group\" },\n    49→      { status: 403 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"Group must be full before creating a Telegram group\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n    61→    return NextResponse.json(\n    62→      { error: \"Telegram link already set\", telegramLink: listing.telegram_link },\n    63→      { status: 400 }\n    64→    );\n    65→  }\n    66→\n    67→  const deepLink = await generateGroupDeepLink(listingId);\n    68→  if (!deepLink) {\n    69→    return NextResponse.json(\n    70→      { error: \"Failed to generate deep link. Check bot configuration.\" },\n    71→      { status: 500 }\n    72→    );\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  return NextResponse.json({\n    76→    deepLink,\n    77→    instructions:\n    78→      \"Click the link to open Telegram and create a group. The bot will automatically generate an invite link for your reading group.\",\n    79→  });\n    80→}\n    81→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"ab850832-5f4d-4a9a-a1c6-8a2406332c76","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:01:02.873Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013s6bAaQqgCTYUyKEufBEFr","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await getSession();\n    11→  if (!session.userId) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json(\n    13→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    14→      { status: 401 }\n    15→    );\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Discord bot is not configured\" },\n    21→      { status: 503 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  const { id } = await params;\n    26→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    27→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  const listing = db\n    32→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    33→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    40→    return NextResponse.json(\n    41→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n    42→      { status: 403 }\n    43→    );\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n    49→      { status: 400 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n    61→\n    62→  return NextResponse.json({\n    63→    inviteUrl,\n    64→    instructions:\n    65→      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n    66→      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n    67→  });\n    68→}\n    69→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"3eaeb49a-34a4-44cc-8690-e961c217adfd","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:01:03.082Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01LH6B8v3ptwJgvo9kxXxdRS","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012NtLCQ9y37Z1AfGHEC1khb","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1209,"cache_read_input_tokens":14446,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1209,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"bc693caf-8390-4e4b-b67e-78e5265c4d0b"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012NtLCQ9y37Z1AfGHEC1khb","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createTextChannel,\n     5→  createChannelInvite,\n     6→  sendChannelMessage,\n     7→} from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     8→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     9→\n    10→/**\n    11→ * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n    12→ *\n    13→ * This endpoint handles:\n    14→ * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n    15→ *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n    16→ *\n    17→ * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n    18→ * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n    19→ * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n    20→ */\n    21→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    22→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    23→  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    24→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    26→  }\n    27→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n    28→  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  try {\n    33→    const body = await req.json();\n    34→    const { type } = body;\n    35→\n    36→    if (type === \"link\") {\n    37→      return handleLink(body);\n    38→    }\n    39→\n    40→    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    41→    if (type === 1) {\n    42→      // PING - respond with PONG\n    43→      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    44→    }\n    45→\n    46→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n    47→  } catch (err) {\n    48→    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    49→    return NextResponse.json(\n    50→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    51→      { status: 500 }\n    52→    );\n    53→  }\n    54→}\n    55→\n    56→async function handleLink(body: {\n    57→  guildId: string;\n    58→  listingId: number;\n    59→  channelId?: string;\n    60→}) {\n    61→  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n    62→\n    63→  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  const listing = db\n    71→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    72→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→  if (!listing) {\n    75→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    76→  }\n    77→\n    78→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    79→    return NextResponse.json(\n    80→      { error: \"Cannot link Discord until the group is full\" },\n    81→      { status: 400 }\n    82→    );\n    83→  }\n    84→\n    85→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    86→    return NextResponse.json(\n    87→      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n    88→      { status: 400 }\n    89→    );\n    90→  }\n    91→\n    92→  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n    93→\n    94→  // If no channel specified, create one\n    95→  if (!targetChannelId) {\n    96→    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n    97→    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n    98→      .toLowerCase()\n    99→      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n   100→      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n   101→\n   102→    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n   103→      guildId,\n   104→      channelName,\n   105→      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n   106→    );\n   107→\n   108→    if (!channel) {\n   109→      return NextResponse.json(\n   110→        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n   111→        { status: 500 }\n   112→      );\n   113→    }\n   114→\n   115→    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n   116→  }\n   117→\n   118→  // Create invite link\n   119→  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n   120→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   121→    return NextResponse.json(\n   122→      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n   123→      { status: 500 }\n   124→    );\n   125→  }\n   126→\n   127→  // Save to DB\n   128→  db.prepare(\n   129→    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   130→  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n   131→\n   132→  // Notify all members\n   133→  const members = db\n   134→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   135→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   136→\n   137→  for (const member of members) {\n   138→    createNotification(\n   139→      member.user_id,\n   140→      listingId,\n   141→      \"discord_ready\",\n   142→      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n   143→    );\n   144→  }\n   145→\n   146→  // Send welcome message to the channel\n   147→  await sendChannelMessage(\n   148→    targetChannelId,\n   149→    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n   150→      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n   151→      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n   152→      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n   153→      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n   154→  );\n   155→\n   156→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n   157→}\n   158→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"96e449e5-626a-4fdd-9f08-39b3eed39149","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:01:03.303Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a16af57fd7aa9856d","tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","description":"Reading app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":15685,"tool_uses":8,"duration_ms":4158},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"1aa61bfd-7bec-4127-baf0-c4341b05d634","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01LH6B8v3ptwJgvo9kxXxdRS","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_013M5ATra1HXKV5Wzw1qScnc","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1209,"cache_read_input_tokens":14446,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1209,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"71fa2ca8-57a0-41c5-bb5e-7a04feee4f4d"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013M5ATra1HXKV5Wzw1qScnc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createChatInviteLink,\n     5→  exportChatInviteLink,\n     6→  sendMessage,\n     7→  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n     8→} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     9→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    10→\n    11→/**\n    12→ * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n    13→ *\n    14→ * Handles two types of updates:\n    15→ *\n    16→ * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n    17→ *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n    18→ *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n    19→ *    and save it to the listing.\n    20→ *\n    21→ * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n    22→ *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n    23→ */\n    24→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    25→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    26→  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    27→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    29→  }\n    30→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n    31→  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    32→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n    36→  try {\n    37→    update = await req.json();\n    38→  } catch {\n    39→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n    40→  }\n    41→\n    42→  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n    43→  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    44→    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n    45→      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    46→    );\n    47→  }\n    48→\n    49→  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n    50→  if (update.message) {\n    51→    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n    52→  }\n    53→\n    54→  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n    55→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    56→}\n    57→\n    58→interface ChatMemberUpdate {\n    59→  chat: {\n    60→    id: number;\n    61→    title?: string;\n    62→    type: string;\n    63→  };\n    64→  from: {\n    65→    id: number;\n    66→    first_name: string;\n    67→  };\n    68→  new_chat_member: {\n    69→    user: {\n    70→      id: number;\n    71→      is_bot: boolean;\n    72→    };\n    73→    status: string;\n    74→  };\n    75→}\n    76→\n    77→interface TelegramMessage {\n    78→  chat: {\n    79→    id: number;\n    80→    type: string;\n    81→    title?: string;\n    82→  };\n    83→  text?: string;\n    84→  from?: {\n    85→    id: number;\n    86→  };\n    87→}\n    88→\n    89→/**\n    90→ * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n    91→ *\n    92→ * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n    93→ * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n    94→ * 1. Creates the group\n    95→ * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n    96→ * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n    97→ *\n    98→ * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n    99→ */\n   100→async function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n   101→  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n   102→\n   103→  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n   104→  if (\n   105→    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n   106→    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n   107→  ) {\n   108→    return;\n   109→  }\n   110→\n   111→  // Only process group chats\n   112→  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n   113→    return;\n   114→  }\n   115→\n   116→  // Try to find the listing this group belongs to.\n   117→  // Strategy 1: Check pending_telegram_groups mapping table\n   118→  const pending = db\n   119→    .prepare(\n   120→      \"SELECT listing_id FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\"\n   121→    )\n   122→    .get(chat.id) as { listing_id: number } | undefined;\n   123→\n   124→  if (pending) {\n   125→    await linkTelegramToListing(pending.listing_id, chat.id);\n   126→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\").run(\n   127→      chat.id\n   128→    );\n   129→    return;\n   130→  }\n   131→\n   132→  // Strategy 2: Check for listings awaiting auto-telegram (most recent unlinked full listing by this author)\n   133→  // We store the chat ID and wait for the /start command or title match\n   134→  // For now, store the mapping and send a welcome message\n   135→  await sendMessage(\n   136→    chat.id,\n   137→    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n   138→      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n   139→      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n   143→  try {\n   144→    db.prepare(\n   145→      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n   146→    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n   147→  } catch {\n   148→    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n   149→  }\n   150→}\n   151→\n   152→/**\n   153→ * Handle messages in group chats.\n   154→ * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n   155→ */\n   156→async function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n   157→  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n   158→    return;\n   159→  }\n   160→\n   161→  const text = message.text.trim();\n   162→\n   163→  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n   164→  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n   165→    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n   166→    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n   167→    if (listingId) {\n   168→      await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   169→      return;\n   170→    }\n   171→  }\n   172→\n   173→  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n   174→  // Security: only show listings authored by a Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   175→  if (text === \"/link\") {\n   176→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   177→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   178→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   179→      return;\n   180→    }\n   181→\n   182→    // Look up the Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   183→    const linkedUser = db\n   184→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   185→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   186→\n   187→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   188→      await sendMessage(\n   189→        message.chat.id,\n   190→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account. \" +\n   191→          \"Please use the Telegram setup link from your Bookmate listing page to link your account first.\"\n   192→      );\n   193→      return;\n   194→    }\n   195→\n   196→    // Only show unlinked listings authored by this verified user\n   197→    const unlinkedListings = db\n   198→      .prepare(\n   199→        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n   200→         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n   201→         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n   202→         AND author_id = ?\n   203→         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n   204→      )\n   205→      .all(linkedUser.user_id) as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n   206→\n   207→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n   208→      await sendMessage(\n   209→        message.chat.id,\n   210→        \"No unlinked reading groups found for your account. The group may already be set up!\"\n   211→      );\n   212→      return;\n   213→    }\n   214→\n   215→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n   216→      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n   217→      return;\n   218→    }\n   219→\n   220→    const listText = unlinkedListings\n   221→      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${l.book_title}\"`)\n   222→      .join(\"\\n\");\n   223→    await sendMessage(\n   224→      message.chat.id,\n   225→      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n   226→    );\n   227→    return;\n   228→  }\n   229→\n   230→  // Handle /link_ID command — require verified ownership\n   231→  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n   232→  if (linkMatch) {\n   233→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   234→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   235→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   236→      return;\n   237→    }\n   238→\n   239→    const linkedUser = db\n   240→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   241→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   242→\n   243→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   244→      await sendMessage(\n   245→        message.chat.id,\n   246→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account.\"\n   247→      );\n   248→      return;\n   249→    }\n   250→\n   251→    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n   252→\n   253→    // Verify this user is the listing author and the listing is full\n   254→    const listing = db\n   255→      .prepare(\"SELECT author_id, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   256→      .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; is_full: number } | undefined;\n   257→\n   258→    if (!listing || listing.author_id !== linkedUser.user_id) {\n   259→      await sendMessage(\n   260→        message.chat.id,\n   261→        \"You can only link Telegram groups to your own Bookmate listings.\"\n   262→      );\n   263→      return;\n   264→    }\n   265→\n   266→    if (!listing.is_full) {\n   267→      await sendMessage(\n   268→        message.chat.id,\n   269→        \"This listing's group is not full yet. You can link a Telegram group once the group reaches its maximum size.\"\n   270→      );\n   271→      return;\n   272→    }\n   273→\n   274→    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   275→  }\n   276→}\n   277→\n   278→/**\n   279→ * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n   280→ * 1. Generate an invite link\n   281→ * 2. Save it to the listing\n   282→ * 3. Notify all members\n   283→ * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n   284→ */\n   285→async function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n   286→  const listing = db\n   287→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   288→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   289→\n   290→  if (!listing) {\n   291→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n   292→    return;\n   293→  }\n   294→\n   295→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n   296→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n   297→    return;\n   298→  }\n   299→\n   300→  // Try to create an invite link\n   301→  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   302→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   303→    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n   304→    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   305→  }\n   306→\n   307→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   308→    await sendMessage(\n   309→      chatId,\n   310→      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n   311→        \"then try again with /link\"\n   312→    );\n   313→    return;\n   314→  }\n   315→\n   316→  // Save to database\n   317→  db.prepare(\n   318→    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   319→  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n   320→\n   321→  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n   322→  const members = db\n   323→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   324→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   325→\n   326→  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n   327→  for (const member of members) {\n   328→    createNotification(\n   329→      member.user_id,\n   330→      listingId,\n   331→      \"telegram_ready\",\n   332→      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n   333→    );\n   334→  }\n   335→\n   336→  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n   337→  await sendMessage(\n   338→    chatId,\n   339→    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${bookTitle}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n   340→      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n   341→      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n   342→  );\n   343→}\n   344→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"3cfb8cad-b58b-4ec1-ab5e-480036423f5b","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:01:03.700Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KHMedp2y7FrDt14mMoTPQ9","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(\n     6→  req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    10→  if (!session) {\n    11→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    12→  }\n    13→\n    14→  const { id } = await params;\n    15→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    16→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    17→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const listing = db\n    21→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    22→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    23→\n    24→  if (!listing) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    26→  }\n    27→\n    28→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json(\n    30→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set the Discord link\" },\n    31→      { status: 403 }\n    32→    );\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json(\n    37→      { error: \"Group must be full before sharing a platform link\" },\n    38→      { status: 400 }\n    39→    );\n    40→  }\n    41→\n    42→  const { discordLink } = await req.json();\n    43→\n    44→  if (\n    45→    !discordLink ||\n    46→    discordLink.length > 512 ||\n    47→    !(\n    48→      discordLink.startsWith(\"https://discord.gg/\") ||\n    49→      discordLink.startsWith(\"https://discord.com/invite/\")\n    50→    )\n    51→  ) {\n    52→    return NextResponse.json(\n    53→      {\n    54→        error:\n    55→          \"Please provide a valid Discord invite link (https://discord.gg/... or https://discord.com/invite/..., max 512 characters)\",\n    56→      },\n    57→      { status: 400 }\n    58→    );\n    59→  }\n    60→\n    61→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    62→    discordLink.trim(),\n    63→    listingId\n    64→  );\n    65→\n    66→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    67→}\n    68→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UhhE6x8LEMNbFw8vzbBcji","session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"0aaa65ca-c795-4e37-af08-b1be618d7966","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:01:03.715Z"}
