/root/Projects/bookmate/app/__tests__/api/discord/discord-webhook.test.ts"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"4d0ecac1-1593-4f7b-b0c3-343456e103de","timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:04:58.502Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/__tests__/api/discord/discord-webhook.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram',\n      discord_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      telegram_chat_id INTEGER,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      listing_id INTEGER,\n      type TEXT NOT NULL,\n      message TEXT NOT NULL,\n      is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockDiscord = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  createTextChannel: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ id: \"ch-123\", name: \"bookmate-test-book\" }),\n  createChannelInvite: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(\"https://discord.gg/abc123\"),\n  sendChannelMessage: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(true),\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/discord\", () => mockDiscord);\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/notifications\", () => ({\n  createNotification: vi.fn(),\n}));\n\nconst { POST } = await import(\"@/app/api/discord/webhook/route\");\n\nfunction insertUser(email = \"test@example.com\", name = \"Test User\") {\n  testDb\n    .prepare(\"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\")\n    .run(email, \"hash\", name);\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\nfunction insertListing(\n  authorId: number,\n  overrides: Partial<{\n    is_full: number;\n    discord_link: string;\n    book_title: string;\n  }> = {}\n) {\n  testDb\n    .prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, is_full, discord_link)\n       VALUES (?, ?, 'Test Author', '1ch/wk', '2026-04-01', 'text', 5, ?, ?)`\n    )\n    .run(authorId, overrides.book_title ?? \"Test Book\", overrides.is_full ?? 0, overrides.discord_link ?? \"\");\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\nfunction addMember(listingId: number, userId: number) {\n  testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, userId);\n}\n\ndescribe(\"POST /api/discord/webhook\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    vi.stubEnv(\"DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"discord-webhook-secret\");\n    mockDiscord.createTextChannel.mockResolvedValue({ id: \"ch-123\", name: \"bookmate-test-book\" });\n    mockDiscord.createChannelInvite.mockResolvedValue(\"https://discord.gg/abc123\");\n    mockDiscord.sendChannelMessage.mockResolvedValue(true);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when webhook secret is not configured\", async () => {\n    vi.stubEnv(\"DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"\");\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"g1\", listingId: 1 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"anything\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 403 when secret header is wrong\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"g1\", listingId: 1 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"wrong\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(403);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 for unknown event type\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"unknown\" },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"responds to Discord PING with PONG\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: 1 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data).toEqual({ type: 1 });\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when guildId or listingId missing for link type\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"\", listingId: 0 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 404 when listing not found\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId: 999 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(404);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when group is not full\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 0 });\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when discord link already set\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1, discord_link: \"https://discord.gg/existing\" });\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"creates channel and links successfully\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data).toHaveProperty(\"ok\", true);\n    expect(data).toHaveProperty(\"inviteLink\", \"https://discord.gg/abc123\");\n\n    // Verify DB was updated\n    const listing = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT discord_link, discord_channel_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as {\n      discord_link: string;\n      discord_channel_id: string;\n    };\n    expect(listing.discord_link).toBe(\"https://discord.gg/abc123\");\n    expect(listing.discord_channel_id).toBe(\"ch-123\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"uses provided channelId instead of creating one\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId, channelId: \"existing-ch\" },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n\n expect(mockDiscord.createChannelInvite).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\"existing-ch\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when channel creation fails\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    mockDiscord.createTextChannel.mockResolvedValue(null);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_chats\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_user_links\");","newString":"    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_chats\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_user_links\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");","originalFile":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram',\n      discord_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      telegram_chat_id INTEGER,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      listing_id INTEGER,\n      type TEXT NOT NULL,\n      message TEXT NOT NULL,\n      is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      PRIMARY KEY (listing_id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n      chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n      chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockTelegram = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  createChatInviteLink: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(\"https://t.me/+invite123\"),\n  exportChatInviteLink: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(null),\n  sendMessage: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(true),\n  parseListingIdFromPayload: vi.fn().mockImplementation((payload: string) => {\n    const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n    return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n  }),\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/telegram\", () => mockTelegram);\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/notifications\", () => ({\n  createNotification: vi.fn(),\n}));\n\nconst { POST } = await import(\"@/app/api/telegram/webhook/route\");\n\nfunction insertUser(email = \"test@example.com\", name = \"Test User\") {\n  testDb\n    .prepare(\"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\")\n    .run(email, \"hash\", name);\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\nfunction insertListing(\n  authorId: number,\n  overrides: Partial<{\n    is_full: number;\n    telegram_link: string;\n    book_title: string;\n  }> = {}\n) {\n  testDb\n    .prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, is_full, telegram_link)\n       VALUES (?, ?, 'Test Author', '1ch/wk', '2026-04-01', 'text', 5, ?, ?)`\n    )\n    .run(authorId, overrides.book_title ?? \"Test Book\", overrides.is_full ?? 0, overrides.telegram_link ?? \"\");\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\nfunction addMember(listingId: number, userId: number) {\n  testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, userId);\n}\n\ndescribe(\"POST /api/telegram/webhook\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_chats\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_user_links\");\n    vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"webhook-secret\");\n    mockTelegram.createChatInviteLink.mockResolvedValue(\"https://t.me/+invite123\");\n    mockTelegram.exportChatInviteLink.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    mockTelegram.sendMessage.mockResolvedValue(true);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when webhook secret is not configured\", async () => {\n    vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"\");\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {},\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"anything\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 403 when secret header is wrong\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {},\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"wrong-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(403);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 200 for empty update (no action)\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { update_id: 1 },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data).toEqual({ ok: true });\n  });\n\n  it(\"handles /start command with listing payload\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 2,\n        message: {\n          chat: { id: -100123, type: \"group\", title: \"Reading Group\" },\n          text: `/start listing_${listingId}`,\n          from: { id: 456 },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n\n    // Verify telegram_link was set\n    const listing = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { telegram_link: string };\n    expect(listing.telegram_link).toBe(\"https://t.me/+invite123\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"handles pending_telegram_groups mapping via my_chat_member\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    // Insert pending mapping\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO pending_telegram_groups (listing_id, telegram_chat_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, -100999);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 3,\n        my_chat_member: {\n          chat: { id: -100999, type: \"group\", title: \"My Group\" },\n          from: { id: 789, first_name: \"Author\" },\n          new_chat_member: {\n            user: { id: 111, is_bot: true },\n            status: \"member\",\n          },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n\n    // Verify listing was linked\n    const listing = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { telegram_link: string };\n    expect(listing.telegram_link).toBe(\"https://t.me/+invite123\");\n\n    // Verify pending mapping was deleted\n    const pending = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT * FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE listing_id = ?\").get(listingId);\n    expect(pending).toBeUndefined();\n  });\n\n  it(\"ignores my_chat_member when bot is not added as member/admin\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 4,\n        my_chat_member: {\n          chat: { id: -100123, type: \"group\" },\n          from: { id: 789, first_name: \"User\" },\n          new_chat_member: {\n            user: { id: 111, is_bot: true },\n            status: \"left\",\n          },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    // No crash, just silent ignore\n  });\n\n  it(\"handles /link command with linked user and single unlinked listing\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    // Link telegram user\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO telegram_user_links (user_id, telegram_user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(userId, 456);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 5,\n        message: {\n          chat: { id: -100555, type: \"group\" },\n          text: \"/link\",\n          from: { id: 456 },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n\n    // Listing should be linked\n    const listing = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { telegram_link: string };\n    expect(listing.telegram_link).toBe(\"https://t.me/+invite123\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"handles /link command from unlinked telegram user\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 6,\n        message: {\n          chat: { id: -100555, type: \"group\" },\n          text: \"/link\",\n          from: { id: 999 },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(mockTelegram.sendMessage).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\n      -100555,\n      expect.stringContaining(\"not linked\")\n    );\n  });\n\n  it(\"handles /link_ID command with verified ownership\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO telegram_user_links (user_id, telegram_user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(userId, 456);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 7,\n        message: {\n          chat: { id: -100666, type: \"group\" },\n          text: `/link_${listingId}`,\n          from: { id: 456 },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n\n    const listing = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { telegram_link: string };\n    expect(listing.telegram_link).toBe(\"https://t.me/+invite123\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"rejects /link_ID from non-author\", async () => {\n    const authorId = insertUser(\"author@test.com\", \"Author\");\n    const otherId = insertUser(\"other@test.com\", \"Other\");\n    const listingId = insertListing(authorId, { is_full: 1 });\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO telegram_user_links (user_id, telegram_user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(otherId, 789);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 8,\n        message: {\n          chat: { id: -100777, type: \"group\" },\n          text: `/link_${listingId}`,\n          from: { id: 789 },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(mockTelegram.sendMessage).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\n      -100777,\n      expect.stringContaining(\"only link Telegram groups to your own\")\n    );\n  });\n\n  it(\"ignores non-group messages\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 9,\n        message: {\n          chat: { id: 123, type: \"private\" },\n          text: \"/link\",\n          from: { id: 456 },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    // No action taken for private chats\n  });\n\n  it(\"uses fallback exportChatInviteLink when createChatInviteLink fails\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    mockTelegram.createChatInviteLink.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    mockTelegram.exportChatInviteLink.mockResolvedValue(\"https://t.me/+fallback\");\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: {\n        update_id: 10,\n        message: {\n          chat: { id: -100888, type: \"supergroup\" },\n          text: `/start listing_${listingId}`,\n          from: { id: 456 },\n        },\n      },\n      headers: { \"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\": \"webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n\n    const listing = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { telegram_link: string };\n    expect(listing.telegram_link).toBe(\"https://t.me/+fallback\");\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":126,"oldLines":13,"newStart":126,"newLines":13,"lines":[" "," describe(\"POST /api/telegram/webhook\", () => {","   beforeEach(() => {","-    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");","-    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");","     testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");","     testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");","     testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups\");","     testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_chats\");","     testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM telegram_user_links\");","+    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");","+    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");","     vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"webhook-secret\");","     mockTelegram.createChatInviteLink.mockResolvedValue(\"https://t.me/+invite123\");","     mockTelegram.exportChatInviteLink.mockResolvedValue(null);"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");","originalFile":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram',\n      discord_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      telegram_chat_id INTEGER,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      listing_id INTEGER,\n      type TEXT NOT NULL,\n      message TEXT NOT NULL,\n      is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockDiscord = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  createTextChannel: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ id: \"ch-123\", name: \"bookmate-test-book\" }),\n  createChannelInvite: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(\"https://discord.gg/abc123\"),\n  sendChannelMessage: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(true),\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/discord\", () => mockDiscord);\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/notifications\", () => ({\n  createNotification: vi.fn(),\n}));\n\nconst { POST } = await import(\"@/app/api/discord/webhook/route\");\n\nfunction insertUser(email = \"test@example.com\", name = \"Test User\") {\n  testDb\n    .prepare(\"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\")\n    .run(email, \"hash\", name);\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\nfunction insertListing(\n  authorId: number,\n  overrides: Partial<{\n    is_full: number;\n    discord_link: string;\n    book_title: string;\n  }> = {}\n) {\n  testDb\n    .prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, is_full, discord_link)\n       VALUES (?, ?, 'Test Author', '1ch/wk', '2026-04-01', 'text', 5, ?, ?)`\n    )\n    .run(authorId, overrides.book_title ?? \"Test Book\", overrides.is_full ?? 0, overrides.discord_link ?? \"\");\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\nfunction addMember(listingId: number, userId: number) {\n  testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, userId);\n}\n\ndescribe(\"POST /api/discord/webhook\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    vi.stubEnv(\"DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"discord-webhook-secret\");\n    mockDiscord.createTextChannel.mockResolvedValue({ id: \"ch-123\", name: \"bookmate-test-book\" });\n    mockDiscord.createChannelInvite.mockResolvedValue(\"https://discord.gg/abc123\");\n    mockDiscord.sendChannelMessage.mockResolvedValue(true);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when webhook secret is not configured\", async () => {\n    vi.stubEnv(\"DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"\");\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"g1\", listingId: 1 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"anything\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 403 when secret header is wrong\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"g1\", listingId: 1 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"wrong\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(403);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 for unknown event type\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"unknown\" },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"responds to Discord PING with PONG\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: 1 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data).toEqual({ type: 1 });\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when guildId or listingId missing for link type\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"\", listingId: 0 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 404 when listing not found\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId: 999 },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(404);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when group is not full\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 0 });\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when discord link already set\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1, discord_link: \"https://discord.gg/existing\" });\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"creates channel and links successfully\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data).toHaveProperty(\"ok\", true);\n    expect(data).toHaveProperty(\"inviteLink\", \"https://discord.gg/abc123\");\n\n    // Verify DB was updated\n    const listing = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT discord_link, discord_channel_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as {\n      discord_link: string;\n      discord_channel_id: string;\n    };\n    expect(listing.discord_link).toBe(\"https://discord.gg/abc123\");\n    expect(listing.discord_channel_id).toBe(\"ch-123\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"uses provided channelId instead of creating one\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    addMember(listingId, userId);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId, channelId: \"existing-ch\" },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n\n    // Should not have created a new channel\n    expect(mockDiscord.createTextChannel).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n\n    // Should have created invite for the existing channel\n    expect(mockDiscord.createChannelInvite).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\"existing-ch\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when channel creation fails\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    mockDiscord.createTextChannel.mockResolvedValue(null);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when invite creation fails\", async () => {\n    const userId = insertUser();\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId, { is_full: 1 });\n    mockDiscord.createChannelInvite.mockResolvedValue(null);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/discord/webhook\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { type: \"link\", guildId: \"guild-1\", listingId },\n      headers: { \"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\": \"discord-webhook-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":105,"oldLines":10,"newStart":105,"newLines":10,"lines":[" "," describe(\"POST /api/discord/webhook\", () => {","   beforeEach(() => {","-    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");","-    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");","     testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");","     testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");","+    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");","+    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");","     vi.stubEnv(\"DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET\", \"discord-webhook-secret\");","     mockDiscord.createTextChannel.mockResolvedValue({ id: \"ch-123\", name: \"bookmate-test-book\" });","     mockDiscord.createChannelInvite.mockResolvedValue(\"https://discord.gg/abc123\");"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

__tests__/api/listings/listing-auto-discord.test.ts\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mGET /api/listings/[id]/auto-discord\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mreturns invite URL and instructions on success\u001b[32m 1\u001b[2mms\u001b[22m\u001b[39m\n \u001b[32m✓\u001b[39m __tests__/api/auth/login.test.ts\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mPOST /api/auth/login\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mlogs in successfully with correct missing\u001b[32m 1\u001b[2mms\u001b[22m\u001b[39m\n \u001b[32m✓\u001b[39m __tests__/api/auth/login.test.ts\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mPOST /api/auth/login\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mreturns 401 for non-existent email\u001b[32m 1\u001b[2mms\u001b[22m\u001b[39m\n \u001b[32m✓\u001b[39m __tests__/api/auth/login.test.ts\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mPOST /api/auth/login\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mreturns 401 for wrong password\u001b[32m 1\u001b[2mms\u001b[22m\u001b[39m\n \u001b[32m✓\u001b[39m __tests__/api/auth/login.test.ts\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mPOST /api/auth/login\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mhandles case-insensitive email lookup\u001b[32m 1\u001b[2mms\u001b[22m\u001b[39m\n \u001b[32m✓\u001b[39m __tests__/api/profile/profile.test.ts\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mPATCH /api/profile\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mreturns 401 when not authenticated\u001b[32m 5\u001b[2mms\u001b[22m\u001b[39m\n \u001b[32m✓\u001b[39m __tests__/api/profile/profile.test.ts\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mPATCH /api/profile\u001b[2m > \u001b[22mupdates display name it(\"returns bot and webhook configuration status\", async () => {\n    const res = await GET();","originalFile":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst mockTelegram = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  isBotConfigured: vi.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\n  setWebhook: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(true),\n  getBotInfo: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ id: 123, username: \"BookmateBot\", first_name: \"Bookmate\" }),\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/telegram\", () => mockTelegram);\n\nconst { POST, GET } = await import(\"@/app/api/telegram/setup/route\");\n\ndescribe(\"POST /api/telegram/setup\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET\", \"test-secret\");\n    vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL\", \"https://example.com/api/telegram/webhook\");\n    mockTelegram.isBotConfigured.mockReturnValue(true);\n    mockTelegram.setWebhook.mockResolvedValue(true);\n    mockTelegram.getBotInfo.mockResolvedValue({ id: 123, username: \"BookmateBot\", first_name: \"Bookmate\" });\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET is not configured\", async () => {\n    vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET\", \"\");\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { secret: \"anything\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 403 when secret is wrong\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { secret: \"wrong-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(403);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when bot is not configured\", async () => {\n    mockTelegram.isBotConfigured.mockReturnValue(false);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { secret: \"test-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL is not configured\", async () => {\n    vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL\", \"\");\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { secret: \"test-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when getBotInfo fails\", async () => {\n    mockTelegram.getBotInfo.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { secret: \"test-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 500 when setWebhook fails\", async () => {\n    mockTelegram.setWebhook.mockResolvedValue(false);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { secret: \"test-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(500);\n  });\n\n  it(\"sets up webhook successfully\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { secret: \"test-secret\" },\n    });\n    const res = await POST(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data).toEqual({\n      ok: true,\n      bot: { username: \"BookmateBot\", id: 123 },\n      webhookUrl: \"https://example.com/api/telegram/webhook\",\n    });\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe(\"GET /api/telegram/setup\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    mockTelegram.isBotConfigured.mockReturnValue(true);\n    vi.stubEnv(\"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL\", \"https://example.com/api/telegram/webhook\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns bot and webhook configuration status\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\");\n    const res = await GET();\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data).toHaveProperty(\"botConfigured\");\n    expect(data).toHaveProperty(\"webhookUrlConfigured\");\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":108,"oldLines":7,"newStart":108,"newLines":6,"lines":["   });"," ","   it(\"returns bot and webhook configuration status\", async () => {","-    const req = createTestRequest(\"http://localhost:3000/api/telegram/setup\");","     const res = await GET();","     const { status, data } = await parseResponse(res);"," {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01VfMyyKRwt6mLrUCuqcJCxo","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Po6fPJVS6GmaiuSDQxcGaG","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\ntest: add coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (#84)\n\n69 new tests across 8 previously untested route files:\n- listings/[id]/telegram (manual link setting)\n- listings/[id]/discord (manual link setting)\n- listings/[id]/auto-telegram (deep link generation)\n- listings/[id]/auto-discord (bot invite URL)\n- telegram/setup (webhook configuration)\n- telegram/webhook (event handler: /start, /link, my_chat_member)\n- discord/setup (bot verification)\n- discord/webhook (channel creation, linking, PING/PONG)\n\nTotal test count: 128 → 197\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Commit test files"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":188,"cache_read_input_tokens":87112,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":188},"output_tokens":51,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"150c5d06-33a9-43a8-bde1-a01918f58d43"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01KnZWuCHJQu932qHwY8ALbi","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01A9yj8wn7HEeE6dtzNmocdq","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh pr create --title \"Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n- Adds 69 new tests across 8 previously untested Telegram and Discord route files\n- Total test count: 128 → 197 (54% increase)\n- All tests pass, lint clean, build passes\n\n### Routes covered:\n- `POST /api/listings/[id]/telegram` — manual Telegram link setting (7 tests)\n- `POST /api/listings/[id]/discord` — manual Discord link setting (8 tests)\n- `GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram` — deep link generation (9 tests)\n- `GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-discord` — bot invite URL (8 tests)\n- `POST/GET /api/telegram/setup` — webhook configuration (8 tests)\n- `POST /api/telegram/webhook` — event handler: /start, /link, /link_ID, my_chat_member (11 tests)\n- `POST /api/discord/setup` — bot verification (5 tests)\n- `POST /api/discord/webhook` — channel creation, linking, PING/PONG (11 tests)\n\nCloses #84\n\n## Test plan\n- [x] All 197 tests pass\n- [x] ESLint clean (zero warnings)\n- [x] Build passes\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2>&1","description":"Create pull request"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":266,"cache_read_input_tokens":87819,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":266},"output_tokens":64,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5f7c6a3e-6bc6-4c2f-a059-70b44d05703c","uuid":"dfb6777e-9404-4e4b-9c22-13dfa3c081a9"}

**listing-telegram.test.ts** (7 tests) — auth, validation, author-only, full-group guard, link format, success\n2. **listing-discord.test.ts** (8 tests) — auth, validation, author-only, full-group guard, link format (discord.gg + discord.com/invite), success\n3. **listing-auto-telegram.test.ts** (9 tests) — auth, bot config, validation, author-only, full-group, already-set, deep link success, generation failure\n4. **listing-auto-discord.test.ts** (8 tests) — auth, bot config, validation, author-only, full-group, already-set, invite URL success\n5. **telegram-setup.test.ts** (8 tests) — secret validation, bot config, webhook URL, getBotInfo failure, setWebhook failure, success, GET status\n6. **telegram-webhook.test.ts** (11 tests) — secret validation, empty update, /start with payload, pending_telegram_groups, /link (linked/unlinked user), /link_ID (owner/non-owner), non-group ignore, invite fallback\n7. **discord-setup.test.ts** (5 tests) — secret validation, bot config, getBotInfo failure, success\n8. **discord-webhook.test.ts** (11 tests) — secret validation, unknown type, PING/PONG, missing params, listing not found, not full, already set, channel creation + linking, provided channelId, creation/invite failure\n\n**Total test count: 128 → 197 (54% increase)**\n\nAll tests pass, ESLint clean, build passes, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~4 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n    21→- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n    22→- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n    23→- pattern)\n    26→  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n    27→  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n    28→  - Skip-to-content link\n    29→  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n    30→  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n    31→  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n    32→\n    33→## Production\n    34→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    35→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n    36→- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    37→- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n    38→- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    39→- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n    40→- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n    41→- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n    42→\n    43→## Open PRs\n    44→- None\n    45→\n    46→## Closed Recently\n    47→- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n    48→- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n    49→- #72: Test coverage for profile genres, reading, reputation routes — merged\n    50→- #71: BookCover component with error fallback — merged + deployed\n    51→- #70: Auto-hide listings with past start dates — merged + deployed\n    52→\n    53→## Open Issues\n    54→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n    55→- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~4 days left\n    56→\n    57→## Important Notes\n    58→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    59→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    60→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    61→- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    62→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n    63→- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n    64→- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n    65→- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n    66→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    67→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    68→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    69→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    70→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    71→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    72→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    73→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    74→- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n    75→- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n    76→- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n    77→- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n    78→\n    79→## Test Suite\n    80→- 126 tests across 14 test files, all passing\n    81→- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit\n    82→- Not yet tested: telegram, discord routes\n    83→- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n    84→- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n    85→\n    86→## Components\n    87→- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n    88→\n    89→## Next Session Priority\n    90→1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n    91→2. telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n- #72: Test coverage for profile genres, reading, reputation routes — merged\n- #71: BookCover component with error fallback — merged + deployed\n- #70: Auto-hide listings with past start dates — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~4 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 126 tests across 14 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit\n- Not yet tested: telegram, discord routes\n- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements\n","numLines":94,"startLine":1,"totalLines":94}}}

Closed Recently\n- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n- #72: Test coverage for profile genres, reading, reputation routes — merged\n- #71: BookCover component with error fallback — merged + deployed\n- #70: Auto-hide listings with past start dates — merged + deployed","newString":"## Closed Recently\n- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged\n- #83: Code review round 5 — duplicate notifications, platform link timing, validation — merged + deployed\n- #81: Code review round 4 — email normalization, application state, rate limiter leak — merged + deployed\n- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n- #72: Test coverage for profile genres, reading, reputation routes — merged\n- #71: BookCover component with error fallback — merged + deployed\n- #70: Auto-hide listings with past start dates — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~4 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 126 tests across 14 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit\n- Not yet tested: telegram, discord routes\n- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":44,"oldLines":11,"newStart":44,"newLines":11,"lines":[" - None"," "," ## Closed Recently","+- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged","+- #83: Code review round 5 — duplicate notifications, platform link timing, validation — merged + deployed","+- #81: Code review round 4 — email normalization, application state, rate limiter leak — merged + deployed"," - #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged"," - #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed","-- #72: Test coverage for profile genres, reading, reputation routes — merged","-- #71: BookCover component with error fallback — merged + deployed","-- #70: Auto-hide listings with past start dates — merged + deployed"," "," ## Open Issues"," - #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged\n- #83: Code review round 5 — duplicate notifications, platform link timing, validation — merged + deployed\n- #81: Code review round 4 — email normalization, application state, rate limiter leak — merged + deployed\n- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~4 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 126 tests across 14 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit\n- Not yet tested: telegram, discord routes\n- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":77,"oldLines":10,"newStart":77,"newLines":9,"lines":[" - PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)"," "," ## Test Suite","-- 126 tests across 14 test files, all passing","-- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit","-- Not yet tested: telegram, discord routes","-- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files","+- 197 tests across 22 test files, all passing","+- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook","+- All API routes now have test coverage"," - @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting"," "," ## Components"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements","newString":"## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes now tested — consider E2E testing, performance, or new feature proposals","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged\n- #83: Code review round 5 — duplicate notifications, platform link timing, validation — merged + deployed\n- #81: Code review round 4 — email normalization, application state, rate limiter leak — merged + deployed\n- #79: Test coverage for listing CRUD, join, create routes — merged\n- #75: Code review fixes — navbar menu, validation, telegram/discord guards — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~4 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 197 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":89,"oldLines":4,"newStart":89,"newLines":4,"lines":[" 1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~4 days remaining, still needs-human"," 2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response"," 3. Check production health","-4. Consider: telegram/discord route test coverage, or other improvements","+4. All routes now tested — consider E2E testing, performance, or new feature proposals"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
